lyrics:
The Culture Deck hangs by a pin
The plastic's worn, the slogans thin
"Integrity. Respect. Empowerment."
The ink has faded, meaning's spent

We used to quote it line by line
Now no one looks. We just align
It's still displayed. It's still on file
We just stopped reading it awhile

There's a crack in the Culture Deck
A tear between the words and their effect
They preach inclusion, cut in waves
Say "You're a leader" on your grave
The values shimmer, soft and fake
Just pretty frosting on the break
You hear the talk. You know the trick
Culture's just... a PowerPoint click

They dropped a deck from somewhere bright
Full of words that sounded right
But roots don't grow on mirrored glass
And slogans fade too fast

You can't plant Eskimo belief
In desert grief
The map they drew don't match our ground
So nothing grows-but we pretend it's found

You can't print truth and tape it up
You can't declare what's never grown
Culture isn't copy-paste
It's what we do when we're alone
It's not your vision. Not your deck
It's what survives your cuts and checks
The words you frame won't save your name
When all we feel is not the same

There's a crack in the Culture Deck
A loss of trust and no respect
You told us truth, then changed the frame
We stayed the same-you sold the name
You sold us vision, sold us pride
Then watched us vanish side by side
What's left ain't culture, mission, brand-
Just faded ink
And empty hands
persona:
## 4Castle

The Future of Rock, The Power of Harmony

**4Castle **is a genre-defying, five-piece supergroup under Maricopa Records, built on a foundation of powerful vocal harmonies, intricate musicianship, and fearless innovation. Rooted in rock, but unbound by limitations, their music fuses elements of progressive rock, funk, Latin rhythms, blues, and folk, delivering a sound that is both technically complex and emotionally resonant.

### **A Band Like No Other**

Each member of **4Castle **brings a distinct voice-both musically and vocally-to the mix:
🎸 **Raven Cortez**(Lead Guitar, Vocals) – A fiery virtuoso with Latin and blues influences, weaving face-melting solos into every performance.
🎸 **Skylar “Sky” Monroe**(Rhythm Guitar, Vocals) – The poetic soul of the band, crafting melodic foundations and lyrically rich storytelling.
🎹 **Jonah Vega**(Keyboards, Vocals) – The sonic architect, layering psychedelic synths, Hammond organ swells, and cinematic orchestrations.
🎸 **Malcolm “Mack” Bishop**(Bass, Vocals) – The groove master, blending funk, jazz, and progressive rock into fluid, deep-pocket basslines.
🥁 **Eli “Fox” Foster **(Drums, Vocals) – The wild heartbeat, pushing the band forward with thunderous fills, polyrhythmic grooves, and explosive energy.

### **What Defines 4Castle?**

- **Multi-Layered Vocal Harmonies**– All five members **sing**, creating **rich, choral-like textures**that elevate every song.

- **Complex Arrangements & Bold Songwriting**– Each track is an **immersive journey**, balancing **technical mastery with raw emotion**.

- **Rhythmic Power & Innovation**– From **tight funk grooves to shifting time signatures**, their music **moves you-literally and figuratively**.

- **Lyrically Challenging Yet Accessible**– Their songs explore **deep themes of life, change, and resilience**, wrapped in **memorable hooks and melodies**.

### **4Castle’s Sound**

Imagine putting Emerson, Lake & Palmer, Heart, Led Zeppelin, Sly Stone, and Carlos Santana in a blender. The result? **4Castle**. Their music pays homage to the greats while pushing rock’s boundaries into the future.

### **Join the Journey**

With Maricopa Records, **4Castle **is set to redefine what a modern rock band can be. Expect genre-bending albums, electrifying performances, and music that resonates across generations.

🚀 Follow **4Castle **as they build their legacy-one song, one harmony, and one unforgettable moment at a time.

[](https://open.spotify.com/artist/4Cmxf7xuTHzf7UahMK2xJh)[](https://music.apple.com/us/artist/4castle/1801052673)![](https://www.maricoparecords.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/5-rooks-in-a-dark-setting-1.png)
## Releases

impression.text: This track opens with a dark, melancholic synth drone and intimate vocals, building a brooding and tense emotional atmosphere. The energy levels shift dramatically during the transition into the chorus, exploding from a sparse, electronic-led verse into a high-octane alternative rock anthem with grinding distorted guitars and impassioned, gritty vocals. Professionally produced, the mix masterfully handles this contrast in density, maintaining excellent vocal clarity even when surrounded by the heavy, wall-of-sound instrumentation.

review.review_text: 4Castle aims its sharpest writing yet at corporate doublespeak, and the conceit holds: "You can't plant Eskimo belief / in desert grief" is the kind of line that earns its place in the chorus. The track works by contrast, opening on a brooding C-minor synth drone and intimate vocals before the chorus detonates into grinding, wall-of-sound guitars at a steady 112 BPM, and the mix keeps the vocal clear through all of it. The closing turn, "What's left ain't culture, mission, brand / Just faded ink / and empty hands," lands the indictment without overplaying it. A highlight, and proof their songwriting can carry a heavy idea without sinking under it.
anchors_used: You can't plant Eskimo belief / in desert grief
synth drone to wall-of-sound guitar contrast
What's left ain't culture, mission, brand / Just faded ink / and empty hands
